info.title: Privacy Automation - Data Mapping Automation
info.description: The Data Mapping Automation APIs are used to manage structured records (assets, vendors, processing activities, and legal entities), define schema attributes, traverse parent–child hierarchies, and create cross-record relationships—including links to personal data.

hostname.description: The OneTrust hostname such as app.onetrust.com, app-eu.onetrust.com, app-de.onetrust.com, app-uk.onetrust.com, app-apac.onetrust.com, trial.onetrust.com, or uat.onetrust.com.

tags.name: Inventory
tags.description: APIs used to create and manage inventory records for supported schema types (processing-activities, vendors, assets, entities).

tags.name: Inventory Hierarchies
tags.description: APIs used to list, link, and unlink child inventories under a root inventory to model organizational or data-flow trees.

tags.name: Inventory Relationships V1
tags.description: APIs used to create, update, and delete bidirectional links between inventories and to manage associations to related items (including personal data).

tags.name: Inventory Schema
tags.description: APIs used to define and maintain schema attributes that shape inventory data collection and validation.

tags.name: Personal Data
tags.description: APIs used to list links to personal-data items for an inventory and update advanced attributes on those associations.

get.description: Use this API to retrieve a list of all existing Data Categories.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Optional filter string to search data categories by name

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Results page to be retrieved (0..N)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Number of records per page (1..50)

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sorting criteria in the format: property(,asc|desc). Default sort order is ascending

get.description: Use this API to retrieve a paginated list of inventories for a given type.

parameters.name: schemaName
parameters.description: The `name` property of the schema. The value can be obtained using the [Get Schemas](/onetrust/reference/getSchemas) API.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter string for searching inventories. Format: 'searchText=value'.

parameters.name: markForDelete
parameters.description: Flag to include soft-deleted inventories.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Results page to be retrieved (0..N)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Number of records per page (1..50)

post.description: Use this API to create an inventory of a particular type.

parameters.name: schemaName
parameters.description: The `name` property of the schema.

parameters.name: generator
parameters.description: Optional generator type for the create operation.

parameters.name: createAsMaster
parameters.description: Flag to create the inventory as a master record.

parameters.name: sourceId
parameters.description: Optional source inventory UUID to copy from.

put.description:
Use this API to update the Inventory if already exists or create a new Inventory otherwise.

### Example Request

`PUT https://{hostname}/api/inventory/v2/inventories/{type}/reference/{externalId}`

`PUT https://trial.onetrust.com/api/inventory/v2/inventories/vendors/reference/43fd3a8d-3f6b-464e-bf43-3f0c6c592442`

parameters.name: schemaName
parameters.description: The `name` property of the schema.

parameters.name: generator
parameters.description: Optional generator type for the operation.

200.description: OK - Inventory successfully created or updated.

429.description:
Too Many Requests. 
For more information, see [API Rate Limits](https://developer.onetrust.com/onetrust/reference/rate-limits-overview).

The number of requests made within the specified period.

put.description: Use this API to update the existing inventory. The body of the update request should contain a single object with fields in the same format as the output of the inventory detail endpoint. Only include the fields that need to be updated.

parameters.name: schemaName
parameters.description: The `name` property of the schema.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of an inventory record.

parameters.name: primaryEntity
parameters.description: Flag to indicate if this is a primary entity update.

delete.description: Use this API to delete an Inventory record.

parameters.name: schemaName
parameters.description: The `name` property of the schema.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of an inventory record.

parameters.name: deleteType
parameters.description: The type of deletion to perform. SOFT marks the record as deleted but retains it, HARD permanently deletes it.

parameters.name: deleteReason
parameters.description: An optional message for auditing indicating why the record was deleted.

post.description: Use this API to create a copy of an existing inventory.

parameters.name: schemaName
parameters.description: The `name` property of the schema.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the inventory record to copy.

parameters.name: inheritFromMaster
parameters.description: Flag to indicate if the copy should inherit attributes from the master inventory.

parameters.name: copyRelated
parameters.description: Flag to indicate if related records should also be copied.

parameters.name: copyAsChild
parameters.description: Flag to indicate if the copy should be created as a child of the original inventory.

The type of relationship between inventory records

controlIds.description: Set of control IDs to be implemented for the given inventory entity

status.description: Implementation status of the controls being added to the inventory. If not provided, defaults to 'Pending'

filters.description: A list of criteria definitions to apply. All criteria in this list are typically combined with a logical AND.

attributeKey.description: The key or path of the attribute to filter on (e.g., 'name', 'status.key', 'customFields.someCustomField').

dataType.description: The data type of the attribute being filtered. (Note: Consider replacing with a more descriptive enum or String if specific integer values have meanings, e.g., 0 for String, 1 for Number, 2 for Date). For now, assuming it's an internal type identifier.

operator.description: The operator to use for comparing the attribute with the provided values (e.g., EQUALS, CONTAINS, GREATER_THAN).

values.description: An array of string values to match against the attribute. For operators like 'IN', multiple values can be provided. For others, typically one value is used.

date.description: A DTO for specifying date values, used when the attribute is a date type and the operator requires date comparison (e.g., DATE_EQUALS, DATE_BETWEEN).

fromDateValue.description: The start date for a date range filter, or the specific date if toDateValue is not provided or is the same. Expected format: YYYY-MM-DD.

toDateValue.description: The end date for a date range filter. If filtering for a single day, this can be the same as fromDateValue or omitted if the operator implies a single date. Expected format: YYYY-MM-DD.
